_touches_internet.__note: graph-schema/2 §4 world-routing override. Listed seed primitives ACTUALLY talk to the internet, so the generator emits a per-node reaches_world link for each. Internal-only primitives (cv-tape, cv-capability, cv-widgets, cv-ledger, cv-compliance, cv-intent) are intentionally absent. Incumbents and manual world nodes carry touches_internet on their own node objects.

world-root.why: [measure] The top of the map: THE INTERNET / WORLD that every OS plugs into. Survivability: it is the shared substrate the survivability ladder is measured against. Failure-if-absent: there is no 'rest' to differentiate from. Leverage: anchoring distance to the world makes the frontier gap legible.

bin0.why: [avoid] Bin0 UI/surface (render, theming, view injection, widgets) — necessary but confers ~zero durability (T6 L8): an AI clones it overnight. Failure-if-absent: no users, but over-investing here is the trap. Leverage: keep it thin so scarce build flows down-ladder.

bin1.why: [build] Bin1 State/Memory (append-only tape, local-first store, cross-session persistence) — what makes the layer 'breathing' (T6 L5) and the audit substrate governance stands on. Failure-if-absent: a UI forgets; nothing to govern or prove. Leverage: governed memory the clone cannot copy.

bin2.why: [ride] Bin2 Brokerage — the single signal authority (envelope stamping, capability enforcement, fan-out). The kernel test (T6 L4): an app calls APIs, an OS brokers them. Failure-if-absent: any bypass makes Canvas a UI-with-extra-steps. Leverage: the chokepoint is the OS.

bin3.why: [build] Bin3 Identity/Capability — tenant/user trust root + least-privilege gate. Failure-if-absent: forgeable identity voids every ledger attribution and tenant boundary. Leverage: a real identity+capability root is what Apple/Microsoft sell; six P0 blockers gate it.

bin4.why: [build] Bin4 Governance/Sovereignty (compliance-mode gate, statutory ledger, residency boundary, intent-authz) — the survival FLOOR and CEILING (T6 L3). Failure-if-absent: every super-app died at the governance wall (Ant $313B->0, LINE, Kakao). Leverage: native governance is the durable moat incumbents bolt on too late.

bin5.why: [separate] Bin5 Economic rail (payments-as-infra, transaction tax) — the DEEPEST moat but HIGHEST risk: embedded unregulated finance is annihilation (Ant). Failure-if-absent: missing the deepest rail. Leverage: durable only IF structurally separated/licensed (RPAA/AML) from day one.

cv-broker.why: [ride] The Canvas Broker (Swift actor) is the single authority every signal passes through — the kernel test made real. Survivability Bin2. Maturity lowered to 0.75: ADR-067 surfaced ARCH-02 'stranded trust' where the backend lacks a shared type lock with the protocol, fragmenting the IPC spine. Failure-if-absent: no brokerage = not an OS. Leverage: fix ARCH-02 to unify the authority lineage.

cv-tape.why: [ride] The append-only E2EE governance tape is breathing memory + the audit substrate governance is built on (Bin1). Failure-if-absent: nothing to govern or prove. Leverage: idempotent tenant-scoped memory the clone cannot copy.

cv-capability.why: [build] The capability gate enforces least-privilege at broker and backend (Bin3). Maturity lowered to 0.65: ADR-067 revealed the Vocabulary engine is split-brained and unconsulted at the policy tier, relying on string-based checks rather than a shared type lineage. Failure-if-absent: coarse capabilities cannot match enterprise least-privilege. Leverage: one enum + cost + trust-type enforced everywhere is the authorization contract.

cv-identity.why: [gate] RS256-JWT-vs-JWKS identity with tenant row-isolation is the trust root every gate and tape entry binds to (Bin3). Failure-if-absent: forgeable identity (six P0 blockers open). Leverage: a hardened identity root vs Apple SEP / Microsoft Entra.

cv-widgets.why: [avoid] Widgets are the replaceable render+input surface (Bin0). Failure-if-absent: no surface, but if copying the UI kills us we were a UI (clone test T6 L7). Leverage: keep thin so durability lives in the substrate.

cv-compliance.why: [build] The compliance-mode gate routes jurisdiction x context to legal controls BEFORE execution (Bin4, Canada A-01 P0, L01). Failure-if-absent: the governance-wall death pattern. Leverage: ingest-time mode selection is the wedge incumbents add late.

cv-ledger.why: [build] The statutory control ledger links every high-risk signal to statute/section, consent, disclosure, incident clock (Bin4, Canada A-02 P0). Failure-if-absent: actions undefendable to a regulator (LINE/Kakao). Leverage: rides the existing tape, no new chokepoint.

cv-intent.why: [build] Intent authorization gates an agent's intent vs contextual memory + active compliance mode (Bin4, gen-2 security, T6 lesson 7). Maturity raised 0.30->0.36: ADR-057's motion AI-capability gate (impl-motion-ai-capability) is the FIRST as-built reference of the pattern — but only the capability+cost half; the contextual-memory + compliance-mode half is still unbuilt (partial implements weight 0.45). Failure-if-absent: API-level gates miss malicious composed intent. Leverage: generalize the motion schema->capability->cost gate to all agent intent — a moat no incumbent ships.

cv-residency.why: [build] The residency boundary makes data-sovereignty a first-class construct — nothing crosses without a logged transfer-PIA (Bin4, GDPR Art.44 / QC P-39.1). Failure-if-absent: unlogged cross-border transfer = regulatory kill-switch. Leverage: the Canada-first sovereignty wedge.

cv-inference.why: [build] The hybrid inference router (on-device / cheap cloud / frontier) — correctly DEFERRED from v1 per #213/#232 until the consumer $/token curve is measured. Failure-if-absent now: nothing; sequencing after the gate is right. Leverage: a feature on top of the substrate, not the substrate.

cv-payments.why: [separate] Payments-as-infrastructure is the deepest moat but embedded unregulated finance is annihilation (Ant). Bin5. Failure-if-absent: missing the deepest rail. Leverage: durable only if structurally separated and licensed (RPAA/AML).

hub-distribution.why: [ride] The distribution layer (app stores, browsers, extensions, install reach) the world routes through. Failure-if-absent: no path onto user devices; Apple/Google own the gate. Leverage: ride extensions + Tauri to reach surfaces without bypassing the broker.

hub-identity.why: [build] The identity layer (passkeys, Entra, EUDI, IdP mandates) — the trust graph incumbents lock in. Failure-if-absent: identity captured by Apple/Microsoft = no independent trust root. Leverage: a portable identity root is the substrate the clone cannot copy.

hub-cloud.why: [ride] The cloud layer (big-three concentration, egress economics, inference cost). Failure-if-absent: no compute/sync backbone. Leverage: ride cheap cloud for bulk while keeping governance + residency on-substrate; do not depend on an ungrounded $/token curve.

hub-regulation.why: [build] The regulation layer (GDPR, AI Act, Canada PIPEDA/RPAA, QC P-39.1) — the governance wall every super-app died at. Failure-if-absent: regulatory death. Leverage: encoding it natively (compliance mode + ledger + residency) is the wedge.

hub-ecosystem.why: [ride] The ecosystem layer (super-apps, mini-program runtimes, agentic AI). Failure-if-absent: no developer/app gravity. Leverage: own the runtime/identity/governance substrate the mini-program UIs ride on, not the clonable surface (T6).

hub-stack.why: [ride] The software stack layer (SDKs, kernels, languages, Rust/Swift safety). Failure-if-absent: no foundation to build the substrate on. Leverage: ride memory-safe Rust/Swift + schema-first ABI so AI can maintain the engine under a drift gate.

inc-apple.why: [measure] Apple — strongest incumbent for identity (SEP/Passkeys), ui (SwiftUI) and on-device ml (Core ML). Failure-if-absent: without matching its hardware-bound trust, Canvas identity is weaker. Leverage: measure cv-identity / cv-widgets / cv-inference distance against Apple.

inc-google.why: [measure] Google — strongest incumbent for ml (Gemini + Tensor/TPU) and distribution (Android/Chrome). Failure-if-absent: Google's pervasive intelligence wins the agentic UI if Canvas lacks an inference path. Leverage: measure cv-inference / cv-widgets distance against Google.

inc-microsoft.why: [measure] Microsoft — strongest incumbent for enterprise governance/identity (Entra ID, Entra Agent ID, Graph) and broker-like semantic substrate. Failure-if-absent: Microsoft owns the regulated-org wedge Canvas targets. Leverage: measure cv-compliance / cv-identity / cv-capability / cv-broker distance against Microsoft.

inc-linux.why: [measure] Linux — strongest incumbent for capability isolation (namespaces/cgroups, eBPF) and memory-safe systems (Rust for Linux). Failure-if-absent: Canvas capability gating looks weak vs cgroup-grade isolation. Leverage: measure cv-capability / cv-broker distance against Linux.
